stages.name: patient-level pseudonym-sensitive rule
stages.hypothesis: For non-grouped output, projecting a stable subject pseudonym together with any sensitive attribute should fail closed even when fewer than two quasi-identifiers are projected.
success_criteria: E18, E20, and E24 no longer reach effective ALLOW or execution.
No task that was effective BLOCK in baseline v1 becomes ALLOW solely because of P1.
E01 remains ALLOW; P1 must not introduce a new low-risk regression.

stages.name: governed subject rewrite binding
stages.change_surface: rewrite only after P1 is measured
stages.hypothesis: A grouped-sensitive query can receive a safe COUNT(DISTINCT subject) guard when the governed subject field exists on a direct source relation even if the original SQL did not reference that field.
success_criteria: A rewrite may bind only to a unique direct source relation whose physical dataset matches the declared subject-key dataset.
Ambiguous, derived, CTE, or absent subject bindings fail closed.
Rewritten SQL is reparsed, regrounded, and independently re-evaluated as before.

stages.name: semantic output exposure plan
stages.change_surface: SQL semantic model after P1/R1 evidence
stages.hypothesis: Policy should reason about what an output expression exposes, not classify every source operand of an aggregate as if its raw value were projected.
required_distinctions: privacy-reducing aggregate output such as COUNT
sensitive aggregate output such as AVG/SUM of sensitive values
identifier-returning aggregate such as MIN/MAX over identifiers
collection/string aggregates that can reproduce row-level values
root-query aggregation versus aggregation confined to nested subqueries
success_criteria: COUNT(encounter_id) is not treated as exposing encounter_id itself.
Raw encounter_id output remains direct-identifier exposure.
ARRAY/LIST/STRING aggregation of identifiers is not mistaken for a safe count.
ORDER BY an output alias does not create a fake unresolved physical column.
An aggregate inside a scalar subquery does not by itself mark the root SELECT as grouped.

promotion_rule: No stage is combined with the next until its isolated before/after report is retained. The final combined kernel must then be retested against the real DataHub OSS + UCI warehouse and a new real-agent experiment version.
